name: Voice-based community media platforms in rural India
description: Research and design of IVR/voice forums (e.g. CGNet Swara, Mobile Vaani, Gram Vaani) that let low-literacy, low-connectivity rural populations contribute and consume citizen journalism and community content. Spans problem discovery, ethnographic design, technical automation of question-answering, content moderation, impact evaluation, and operational scale-up of these participatory audio networks.

name: Community radio operations and automation
description: Building and sustaining community radio stations through technical infrastructure such as automation systems, answering machines, IVR integration, and content distribution architectures. Focuses on the operational, commercialization, and design challenges of running small-scale broadcast media in resource-constrained rural settings.

name: Computational detection and analysis of media bias and political polarization
description: Quantitative and NLP-driven methods to measure media bias, polarization, virality, and partisan structure in news coverage, blogs, and social platforms. Includes crowdsourced content analysis, aspect-level bias quantification, blogosphere/Twitter network analysis, and applications to policy discourse in India.

name: Community networks and grassroots connectivity infrastructure
description: Efforts to build locally-owned and operated network infrastructure for underserved communities, including sustainability models like community currencies, gender dynamics of maintenance, and connectivity for small nonprofits. Draws on cs fundamentals, ethnographic design, and operations/scale considerations for last-mile access.

name: WhatsApp and social media adoption in the Global South
description: Studies of how messaging apps, Facebook, and mobile media are adopted and given social meaning by users in developing contexts, including rural sentiment, urban self-empowerment, and misinformation dynamics. Examines actor-networks, filter bubbles, and the drivers and consequences of fake news among marginalized populations.

name: Gendered access to mobile phones and voice interfaces
description: Research on how gender shapes access to, control over, and experience of mobile technology and voice-based services, including IVR experiments on voice gender/accent, women livestock keepers, and gatekeeping of women's mobile internet use. Focuses on marginalization axes that mediate participation in information ecosystems.

name: Women-led participatory media initiatives
description: Media platforms designed and run by and for women in rural, patriarchal contexts (e.g. Khabar Lahariya, women-led participatory audio platforms). Explores the design, sustainability, and empowerment dynamics of gender-centric community media.

name: AI-assisted content moderation and monitoring for climate/environmental discourse
description: Machine learning frameworks for categorizing, moderating, and monitoring media content on climate and environmental conservation topics, often in low-resource languages. Combines cs fundamentals with impact evaluation of AI systems applied to public discourse.

name: Media and information access for displaced and disabled populations
description: Studies of how highly marginalized groups—refugees seeking democratic voice, visually impaired IVR users—access and engage with information systems and civic participation. Centers on populations at the extreme margins of the information ecosystem.
